purpose: Sourced candidate list for high-resonance / mesoscopic resonance-native regimes under PG1-PG8 physical-regime source-intake criteria. This is a candidate queue for future source review, not source extraction or substrate selection.

critical_review_prework.hard_bottleneck_attacked: high-resonance bridge uncertainty: whether mesoscopic resonance-native regimes can supply persistent support identity, native relay/transport, intrinsic readout, and harnessability without collapsing into ordinary controlled resonance hardware
thin_stick_risks: high-Q resonance treated as native settling law
topological protection treated as Formation Medium readout
coupled-mode experiments treated as persistent support/channel identity
source-reviewed platforms treated as engineering feasibility
critical_review_prework.negative_progress_risk: Could become a trendy photonics/cavity-magnonics shopping list unless every candidate carries kill gates and remains external-review only.
critical_review_prework.why_not_convenient_drift: It turns Q5/PRI-001 into specific source classes that can be killed, demoted, or selected for later ingestion under PG1-PG8.

anti_flattening_rule: High-resonance is a possible bridge comparator. It is not CRBSM, not Route A, not scattering-native winner status, not selected substrate, and not native settling law by default.

candidate_classes.label: photonic bound states in the continuum / topological BICs
candidate_classes.why_candidate: BIC and topological-BIC systems directly address long-lived resonant modes, high-Q confinement, boundary/edge/corner localization, and mode tunability in open photonic structures.
kill_notes: high-Q confinement is not native settling law
BIC far-field/topological signatures are not canonical Formation Medium readout
mode localization can remain ordinary photonic engineering
needs distinction between intrinsic mode identity and externally measured resonance

candidate_classes.label: topological polariton / topological laser arrays
candidate_classes.why_candidate: Topological polariton and laser-array systems offer active, mesoscopic, resonant transport platforms with robust edge modes, gain, and accessible readout.
kill_notes: robust lasing mode is not Formation Medium readout
topological transport with gain can still be ordinary photonic routing
single-mode emission is not persistent support ledger
active control may replace rather than expose native dynamics

candidate_classes.label: cavity magnon-polariton / magnon-phonon-photon hybrid systems
candidate_classes.why_candidate: Cavity magnonics and magnon-phonon-photon hybrids directly address mesoscopic mode identity, strong coupling, non-Hermitian mode coalescence, coherent perfect absorption, and hybrid carrier/transport possibilities.
kill_notes: strong coupling is not relay semantics
exceptional points and coherent perfect absorption are not canonical readout
hybrid mode identity may be control-frame dependent
requires evidence that channel identity is native rather than instrument-tracked

candidate_classes.label: mechanical / phononic bound-state-in-continuum resonators
candidate_classes.why_candidate: Mechanical BIC resonators test whether high-Q resonance and protected mode identity can appear in non-optical, mesoscopic mechanical media with potential lab tractability.
kill_notes: high-Q mechanical resonance can be ordinary oscillator engineering
mechanical BIC does not imply Formation Medium readout
needs source evidence for native admissibility and bulk/readout separation
likely negative-control candidate unless readout semantics sharpen

source_candidates.title: Observation of trapped light within the radiation continuum
source_candidates.track_relevance: foundational experimental photonic BIC / trapped light in radiation continuum
source_candidates.candidate_value: persistent high-Q resonant mode clue for PG4 and harnessable photonic platform clue for PG7
source_candidates.blocked_inference: photonic BIC is not Formation Medium readout or selected substrate

source_candidates.title: Lasing action from photonic bound states in continuum
source_candidates.track_relevance: active BIC laser / high-Q open resonant cavity
source_candidates.candidate_value: tests whether resonant mode identity can persist under gain and device operation
source_candidates.blocked_inference: BIC lasing is not native settling or canonical readout

source_candidates.title: Observation of edge bound states in the continuum at truncated silicon pillar photonic crystal
source_candidates.track_relevance: edge BIC in silicon photonic crystal with tunability/surface sensitivity
source_candidates.candidate_value: boundary/edge resonance clue for PG4, PG5, and PG7 source review
source_candidates.blocked_inference: edge BIC sensitivity is not intrinsic Formation Medium readout

source_candidates.title: Nonlinear control of photonic higher-order topological bound states in the continuum
source_candidates.track_relevance: higher-order topological BIC corner states and nonlinear coupling
source_candidates.candidate_value: combines corner/boundary mode, BIC persistence, and controlled coupling in one photonic platform
source_candidates.blocked_inference: corner BIC mode is not a canonical positive-geometric readout

source_candidates.title: Exciton-polariton topological insulator
source_candidates.track_relevance: topological polariton platform with light-matter carrier and edge transport
source_candidates.candidate_value: hybrid quasiparticle carrier clue for PG1 and transport/topology clue for PG3
source_candidates.blocked_inference: topological polariton edge transport is not Formation Medium substrate selection

source_candidates.title: Topological insulator laser: Experiments
source_candidates.track_relevance: active topological laser array with robust edge-mode lasing
source_candidates.candidate_value: tests active topological resonant transport and gain-harnessability under PG3/PG7
source_candidates.blocked_inference: topological laser robustness is not CRBSM relay identity

source_candidates.title: Room temperature electrically pumped topological insulator lasers
source_candidates.track_relevance: room-temperature electrically pumped topological laser array
source_candidates.candidate_value: harnessability and active resonant platform clue for PG7
source_candidates.blocked_inference: electrically pumped topological laser is not engineering-ready Formation Medium

source_candidates.title: Observation of the exceptional point in cavity magnon-polaritons
source_candidates.track_relevance: non-Hermitian cavity magnon-polariton platform with tunable mode coalescence
source_candidates.candidate_value: mode identity, coupling, and non-Hermitian transition clue for PG3/PG4
source_candidates.blocked_inference: exceptional point is not intrinsic readout semantics

source_candidates.title: Cavity-magnon polaritons strongly coupled to phonons
source_candidates.track_relevance: triple strong coupling among photons, magnons, and phonons with long-lived phonons/cooperativity
source_candidates.candidate_value: hybrid carrier/transport and persistent mode identity candidate for PG1/PG3/PG4
source_candidates.blocked_inference: triple strong coupling is not native Formation Medium formation law

source_candidates.title: Strong magnon–photon coupling enhanced by photonic lattice flat-bands
source_candidates.track_relevance: flat-band-enhanced magnon-photon coupling in photonic lattice platform
source_candidates.candidate_value: flat-band mediated high-resonance coupling clue for PG1/PG3/PG4
source_candidates.blocked_inference: flat-band enhancement is not source-faithful Formation Medium substrate evidence

source_candidates.title: Merging mechanical bound states in the continuum in high-aspect-ratio phononic crystal gratings
source_candidates.candidate_value: non-optical resonant persistence candidate for PG4 and negative-control comparison
source_candidates.blocked_inference: mechanical BIC is not intrinsic canonical readout

next_action_policy.preferred_next: choose one candidate class for narrow source review under PG1-PG8, likely HR-C3 if targeting persistent support/channel identity or HR-C1 if targeting boundary/resonant readout
do_not_do_next: move sources into SOURCE_EXTRACTED_CLAIMS without ingestion and extraction
treat high-Q modes as native settling law
treat topological photonic readout as canonical readout
confuse Route A source review with high-resonance-native validation

non_promotion_rule: This PRI-001 artifact is a sourced candidate list only. It does not ingest sources into the repo corpus, extract source claims, validate high-resonance regimes, validate Route A, validate CRBSM, beat scattering-native comparison, select a substrate, prove a settling law, or establish engineering readiness.
